test_cases.name: happy_path_standard_business_presentation
test_cases.scenario: A product manager prepares a 60-minute internal presentation on cloud migration strategy to a mixed audience of CFOs, CTOs, and engineering managers.

input.audience_description: 50 engineering managers and 10 finance leaders from Fortune 500 companies. Expertise: intermediate to expert. Pain points: technical debt, cost optimization, team retention. Interests: scalability, ROI, risk mitigation.
input.presentation_context: Internal meeting, 60 minutes, in-person, 60-person audience, no live demos
input.known_constraints: No proprietary data, must comply with GDPR

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill executes all 8 phases successfully, producing a complete presentation_architecture object with all required components: narrative arc with time allocations, 3-5 key messages mapped to stakeholder types, stakeholder strategies for CFO and CTO, anticipated objections with counter-arguments, Q&A preparation with 5-8 questions, visual strategy with metaphors and design guidance, speaker notes with tone and pacing, and post-presentation follow-up strategy.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Validate Topic, Audience Profile, and Context
Phase 2: Analyze Stakeholders and Prioritize Messages
Phase 3: Generate Narrative Arc
Phase 4: Identify Key Messages
Phase 5: Anticipate Objections and Develop Counter-Arguments
Phase 6: Prepare Q&A Responses and Follow-Up Tactics
Phase 7: Design Visual Strategy
Phase 8: Assemble and Validate Presentation Architecture

test_cases.name: happy_path_executive_pitch
test_cases.scenario: A startup founder pitches Series B funding to venture capitalists in a 30-minute virtual presentation.

input.presentation_topic: Series B Funding Pitch: AI-Powered Supply Chain Optimization
input.audience_description: 8 venture capitalists with deep tech and supply chain expertise. Pain points: market validation, unit economics, competitive differentiation. Interests: market size, team capability, exit potential.
input.presentation_context: Virtual pitch, 30 minutes, 8-person audience, high-stakes decision meeting
input.known_constraints: No unrealistic projections, must cite market research

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill produces a focused presentation_architecture optimized for 30-minute format with compressed time allocations (Act 1: 3-5 min, Act 2: 15-20 min, Act 3: 3-5 min). Key messages emphasize market size, competitive advantage, and team strength. Anticipated objections address unit economics and market validation. Q&A preparation includes likely investor questions about traction and burn rate.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Validate Topic, Audience Profile, and Context
Phase 3: Generate Narrative Arc
Phase 4: Identify Key Messages
Phase 5: Anticipate Objections and Develop Counter-Arguments
Phase 6: Prepare Q&A Responses and Follow-Up Tactics
Phase 8: Assemble and Validate Presentation Architecture

test_cases.name: edge_case_sparse_audience_description
test_cases.scenario: User provides minimal audience information: only 'business people' with no role, expertise level, or pain points specified.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 1 detects sparse audience description and applies default inference rules: assumes 20-50 people, mixed expertise (novice to intermediate), infers pain points from generic business role (cost, efficiency, risk), assumes mixed stakeholder types with at least one decision-maker and one implementer. All assumptions are documented in audience_profile.assumptions. Skill proceeds with documented assumptions and produces complete presentation_architecture.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Validate Topic, Audience Profile, and Context
Phase 3: Generate Narrative Arc
Phase 4: Identify Key Messages
Phase 5: Anticipate Objections and Develop Counter-Arguments
Phase 8: Assemble and Validate Presentation Architecture

test_cases.name: edge_case_vague_topic
test_cases.scenario: User provides a single-word, vague topic: 'presentation' with no context about what aspect to cover.

input.audience_description: 50 marketing professionals, intermediate expertise, interested in campaign effectiveness

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 1 detects vague topic ('presentation' is too generic) and requests clarification with prompt: 'What specific aspect of presentation will you cover? Who is the decision-maker or stakeholder you are trying to influence?' Skill does not proceed to Phase 2 until topic is clarified. Output includes topic_validated: false and clarification_request message.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Validate Topic, Audience Profile, and Context

test_cases.name: edge_case_missing_presentation_context
test_cases.scenario: User omits presentation_context entirely; skill should apply default values.

input.audience_description: Executive leadership team: CEO, CFO, COO. Expert level. Pain points: quarterly performance, strategic alignment.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 1 detects missing presentation_context and applies defaults: 'internal meeting, 60 minutes, in-person, no constraints.' Assumption is documented in presentation_context_parsed.assumptions. Phase 3 uses default 60-minute time allocations: Act 1 (5-10 min), Act 2 (30-40 min), Act 3 (5-10 min), with 10-15% Q&A buffer. Skill produces complete presentation_architecture with documented assumptions.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Validate Topic, Audience Profile, and Context
Phase 3: Generate Narrative Arc
Phase 4: Identify Key Messages
Phase 5: Anticipate Objections and Develop Counter-Arguments
Phase 8: Assemble and Validate Presentation Architecture

test_cases.name: error_handling_conflicting_stakeholders
test_cases.scenario: Audience contains CFO (focused on cost-cutting) and CTO (focused on innovation investment) with fundamentally conflicting goals.

input.audience_description: CFO, CTO, VP Engineering, Head of Product. CFO concerned about cost and ROI. CTO concerned about technical innovation and competitive advantage. VP Engineering concerned about team capability. Head of Product concerned about time-to-market.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 1 detects multiple stakeholder types with conflicting priorities and sets stakeholder_conflict_flag: true. Phase 2 executes stakeholder analysis: identifies CFO (cost metric), CTO (innovation metric), VP Engineering (capability metric), Head of Product (speed metric). Generates bridging messages that acknowledge both cost concerns and innovation needs (e.g., 'AI platform requires upfront investment but reduces long-term operational costs and enables innovation'). Phase 4 ensures key messages address all stakeholder types. Phase 5 generates objections specific to each type. Phase 6 includes Q&A for each stakeholder type.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Validate Topic, Audience Profile, and Context
Phase 2: Analyze Stakeholders and Prioritize Messages
Phase 3: Generate Narrative Arc
Phase 4: Identify Key Messages
Phase 5: Anticipate Objections and Develop Counter-Arguments
Phase 6: Prepare Q&A Responses and Follow-Up Tactics
Phase 8: Assemble and Validate Presentation Architecture

test_cases.name: error_handling_speculative_objections
test_cases.scenario: Audience description provides minimal context; skill cannot identify realistic objections and must generate speculative ones.

input.audience_description: Product team, size unknown, expertise unknown

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 5 detects insufficient context to identify realistic objections. Generates speculative objections based on common product launch concerns: 'Will this work with our existing systems?', 'Do we have the skills to launch this?', 'Is the timeline realistic?'. Documents that objections are speculative in anticipated_objections metadata. Provides counter-arguments for each speculative objection. Skill completes successfully with documented limitations.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Validate Topic, Audience Profile, and Context
Phase 3: Generate Narrative Arc
Phase 4: Identify Key Messages
Phase 5: Anticipate Objections and Develop Counter-Arguments
Phase 6: Prepare Q&A Responses and Follow-Up Tactics
Phase 8: Assemble and Validate Presentation Architecture

test_cases.name: quality_gate_time_allocation_boundary
test_cases.scenario: Presentation context specifies 30 minutes; skill must allocate time precisely to fit within constraint with Q&A buffer.

input.audience_description: IT security team, 20 people, expert level, concerned about incident response time and team readiness
input.presentation_context: 30 minutes, in-person, no live demos, tight schedule
input.known_constraints: Must include incident response workflow diagram

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 3 allocates time strictly within 30-minute constraint: Act 1 (3-5 min), Act 2 (15-20 min), Act 3 (3-5 min), Q&A buffer (2-3 min). Total time_minutes sums to 30 with 10% buffer. Quality gate verifies time allocations are valid and sum correctly. Narrative arc is compressed but complete. Opening hook is concise. Key messages are prioritized for maximum impact in limited time. Q&A preparation focuses on most likely questions.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Validate Topic, Audience Profile, and Context
Phase 3: Generate Narrative Arc
Phase 4: Identify Key Messages
Phase 5: Anticipate Objections and Develop Counter-Arguments
Phase 6: Prepare Q&A Responses and Follow-Up Tactics
Phase 8: Assemble and Validate Presentation Architecture

test_cases.name: quality_gate_stakeholder_coverage
test_cases.scenario: Multiple stakeholder types present; quality gate verifies all types are addressed in key messages, objections, and Q&A.

input.audience_description: HR Director (cost-focused), Employee Wellness Champion (engagement-focused), CFO (ROI-focused), CEO (strategic alignment-focused). Mixed expertise. Pain points vary by role.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 2 identifies four distinct stakeholder types with different success metrics: HR Director (cost control), Wellness Champion (employee engagement), CFO (ROI), CEO (strategic alignment). Phase 4 ensures each key message addresses at least one stakeholder type. Phase 5 generates objections specific to each type (e.g., HR: cost concerns; Wellness Champion: engagement impact; CFO: ROI timeline; CEO: strategic fit). Phase 6 includes at least one Q&A question per stakeholder type. Phase 8 validation checklist confirms all four types are addressed in key messages, objections, and Q&A.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Validate Topic, Audience Profile, and Context
Phase 2: Analyze Stakeholders and Prioritize Messages
Phase 3: Generate Narrative Arc
Phase 4: Identify Key Messages
Phase 5: Anticipate Objections and Develop Counter-Arguments
Phase 6: Prepare Q&A Responses and Follow-Up Tactics
Phase 8: Assemble and Validate Presentation Architecture

test_cases.name: quality_gate_internal_consistency
test_cases.scenario: Quality gate validates that all components of presentation_architecture are internally consistent: narrative arc supports key messages, key messages are addressed in Q&A, objections have counter-arguments, visuals reinforce messages.

input.audience_description: Department heads, 15 people, intermediate expertise, concerned about team stability and career impact
input.known_constraints: Must maintain confidentiality, no specific layoff numbers

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 8 executes comprehensive internal consistency check: (1) Narrative arc opening hook and Act 1 setup directly reference audience pain points (team stability, career impact). (2) Each key message is addressed in Q&A responses. (3) Each anticipated objection has a substantive counter-argument. (4) Visual strategy includes visuals for each key message. (5) Speaker notes provide tone guidance (empathetic, transparent) and pacing cues. (6) Post-presentation follow-up includes resources for career transition support. (7) Validation checklist confirms all components are present and aligned. If any inconsistency is detected, Phase 8 loops back to relevant phase to fix.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Validate Topic, Audience Profile, and Context
Phase 3: Generate Narrative Arc
Phase 4: Identify Key Messages
Phase 5: Anticipate Objections and Develop Counter-Arguments
Phase 6: Prepare Q&A Responses and Follow-Up Tactics
Phase 7: Design Visual Strategy
Phase 8: Assemble and Validate Presentation Architecture

critical_surfaces_covered: Stakeholder type coverage in key messages, objections, and Q&A
